title: thymus longedentatus
side_effects: Thymus longedentatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing excessive salivation, gastrointestinal upset, and skin irritation in some individuals. In rare cases, overconsumption of the plant can lead to allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis and respiratory distress. Additionally, improper use of Thymus longedentatus may cause interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to adverse effects. If not used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, the plant's active compounds may cause liver damage and other serious health issues.
